title: Pinecone Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Pinecone** is a managed vector database. Its REST API is intentionally split across two planes — a control plane for index/collection CRUD, and a per-index data plane for vector operations — and this connector hides that split behind a single Python surface.

| Plane | Base URL | Owns |
|---|---|---|
| **Control plane** | `https://api.pinecone.io` | `list_indexes`, `describe_index`, `create_index`, `delete_index`, `configure_index`, `list_collections`, `create_collection`, `delete_collection` |
| **Data plane** | `https://{index_host}` (per index) | `upsert_vectors`, `query`, `fetch_vectors`, `update_vector`, `delete_vectors`, `describe_index_stats`, `list_namespaces` |

`PineconeConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "pinecone"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) resolves the `index_name → data_plane_host` mapping lazily on first call (via `describe_index`) and caches it on the HTTP client. Subsequent vector ops skip the control plane entirely.

SOC enforced: `connector.py` orchestrates only; all HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::PineconeHTTPClient`; index-summary normalisation in `helpers/normalizer.py`. OCP enforced: every user-requested operation is a standalone `async def`; new operations are added without touching `BaseConnector` or existing methods.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Get a Pinecone API key

1. Sign in at [app.pinecone.io](https://app.pinecone.io).
2. Pick the project the connector will operate on (an API key is scoped to one project).
3. Open **API Keys** in the left sidebar.
4. Click **+ Create API key**, give it a name, and copy the value — Pinecone shows it only once.
5. Copy the **Environment** tag from Project Settings (e.g. `us-east-1-aws`).
6. (Optional) Copy the **Project ID** if your dashboard exposes one.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Pinecone**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 4 |
| `environment` (string) | Environment tag from step 5 |
| `project_id` (string, optional) | Project ID from step 6 |
| `default_index` (string, optional) | Index name used when a call omits `index_name` |
| `default_namespace` (string, optional) | Namespace used when a call omits `namespace` |
| `control_url` (string, optional) | Override `https://api.pinecone.io` (rare) |
| `api_version` (string, optional) | Pin to `2025-01` for stability |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Soft cap, default 100 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `PineconeHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists indexes via the control plane (`GET /indexes`). On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401/403 → `DEGRADED + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Pinecone REST API uses **server-to-server API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent in the **`Api-Key` header**, NOT `Authorization`. This is the Pinecone-specific gotcha — sending the key as a `Bearer ...` value silently 401s.
- The same `Api-Key` value is used for both the control plane and every data-plane host.
- `X-Pinecone-API-Version` pins the API contract so future Pinecone changes do not break your tenant.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key` is non-empty. Initialises `PineconeHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Pinecone endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a synthetic `TokenInfo(access_token=api_key, token_type="ApiKey")` so the platform's post-install flow stays uniform across auth types. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /indexes` — minimal control-plane probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request — control AND data plane — carries:

```
Api-Key:                 <api_key>           ← raw key, NOT Authorization
Content-Type:            application/json
Accept:                  application/json
X-Pinecone-API-Version:  <api_version>       ← default 2025-01
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `Api-Key` header value |
| `environment` | string | yes | `self.environment` | Region tag (e.g. `us-east-1-aws`) — fallback for legacy hosts |
| `project_id` | string | no | `self.project_id` | Project UUID — fallback host builder |
| `default_index` | string | no | `self.default_index` | Per-call default when `index_name` is omitted |
| `default_namespace` | string | no | `self.default_namespace` | Per-call default when `namespace` is omitted |
| `control_url` | string | no | `self.control_url` (default `https://api.pinecone.io`) | Override for proxies |
| `api_version` | string | no | `self.api_version` (default `2025-01`) | `X-Pinecone-API-Version` header value |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key", "environment"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DEFAULT_CONTROL_URL = "https://api.pinecone.io"
_DEFAULT_API_VERSION = "2025-01"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `PineconeConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::PineconeHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; initialise `PineconeHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
Returns a synthetic `TokenInfo` wrapping the configured API key — there is no OAuth code exchange.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists indexes via `GET /indexes`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Discovers the tenant's indexes (via `list_indexes` when no `default_index`, otherwise just the default), then `describe_index` + `describe_index_stats` per index, normalises each to a `NormalizedDocument`, and ingests via `ingest_document`. Pinecone is a vector sink, not a content source — each ingested doc is a per-index summary.

children.title: Indexes (control plane)
children.content:
### `async list_indexes() -> Dict`
`GET /indexes` — list every index in this project.

### `async describe_index(index_name) -> Dict`
`GET /indexes/{name}` — fetch spec including `host`. The HTTP client caches the `index → host` map so subsequent vector ops skip the control plane.

### `async create_index(name, dimension, metric="cosine", cloud="aws", region="us-east-1") -> Dict`
`POST /indexes` — body: `{name, dimension, metric, spec: {serverless: {cloud, region}}}`.

### `async delete_index(index_name) -> Dict`
`DELETE /indexes/{name}` — also clears the cached host.

### `async configure_index(index_name, replicas=None, pod_type=None) -> Dict`
`PATCH /indexes/{name}` — reconfigure pod-based indexes. Body: `{spec: {pod: {replicas?, pod_type?}}}`. At least one of `replicas` / `pod_type` is required; otherwise raises `PineconeBadRequestError`.

children.title: Collections (snapshots)
children.content:
### `async list_collections() -> Dict`
`GET /collections`.

### `async create_collection(name, source) -> Dict`
`POST /collections` — body: `{name, source: <index_name>}`.

### `async delete_collection(collection_name) -> Dict`
`DELETE /collections/{name}`.

children.title: Vectors (data plane)
children.content:
All data-plane methods resolve the per-index host via `describe_index` on first call and then hit `{host}/...` directly.

### `async upsert_vectors(index_name, vectors, namespace="") -> Dict`
`POST {host}/vectors/upsert` — body: `{vectors: [...], namespace?}`. Auto-batches into chunks of 100 (Pinecone caps a single upsert at 1000).

Each vector dict must shape `{"id": str, "values": [float...], "metadata": {...}?}`.

### `async query(index_name, vector, top_k=10, namespace="", filter=None, include_metadata=True, include_values=False) -> Dict`
`POST {host}/query` — body: `{vector, topK, namespace?, filter?, includeMetadata, includeValues}`.

### `async fetch_vectors(index_name, ids, namespace="") -> Dict`
`GET {host}/vectors/fetch?ids=...&namespace=...`.

### `async update_vector(index_name, id, values=None, metadata=None, namespace="") -> Dict`
`POST {host}/vectors/update` — body: `{id, values?, setMetadata?, namespace?}`. Partial update.

### `async delete_vectors(index_name, ids=None, delete_all=False, namespace="") -> Dict`
`POST {host}/vectors/delete` — body: `{ids? | deleteAll?, namespace?}`.

### `async describe_index_stats(index_name, filter=None) -> Dict`
`POST {host}/describe_index_stats` — returns `{dimension, totalVectorCount, namespaces, indexFullness}`.

### `async list_namespaces(index_name) -> Dict`
`GET {host}/namespaces` — falls back to `describe_index_stats` when the dedicated endpoint returns 404 on older indexes.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
PineconeError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── PineconeAuthError                # 401 / 403
├── PineconeBadRequestError          # 400
├── PineconeNotFoundError            # 404
├── PineconeConflictError            # 409
├── PineconeRateLimitError           # 429 — retry_after_s
└── PineconeServerError              # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
PineconeNetworkError = PineconeServerError      # legacy name from older code
PineconeNotFound     = PineconeNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request_with_retry`)

| Status / Error | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 | Raise `PineconeBadRequestError` immediately |
| 401 / 403 | Raise `PineconeAuthError` immediately |
| 404 | Raise `PineconeNotFoundError` immediately |
| 409 | Raise `PineconeConflictError` immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `min(base * 2^attempt + jitter, 32s)`, up to `_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff |
| `httpx.HTTPError` | Wrapped in `PineconeNetworkError`, backoff, retry up to 3 |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches the typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401/403 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
Network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  CONNECTED)
other   → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

`sync()` catches per-index exceptions and returns `SyncStatus.PARTIAL` with `documents_failed > 0` rather than failing the whole sync — visibility wins over all-or-nothing.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Re-enter the key in ACP. Pinecone shows the key only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 401/403 from Pinecone | Verify the API key still exists in Pinecone Console → API Keys, and that it was created for the same project as `environment`. |
| `pinecone.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (default 100 req/min on serverless) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or upgrade the Pinecone plan. |
| 404 on `describe_index` | Index name typo or wrong project | Run `list_indexes` first; copy the exact `name`. |
| `No index specified and no default_index configured` | Caller omitted `index_name` and no default was set | Pass `index_name` explicitly or set `default_index` install_field. |
| `describe_index returned no 'host' field` | Legacy pod index, control plane returned spec without host | Confirm the index is provisioned in the same project as the key; set `environment` + `project_id` so the connector can build a fallback host. |
| Vector upserts succeed but `totalVectorCount` is 0 | Wrong namespace | Confirm the namespace matches the one used at insert time; use `list_namespaces` to inspect. |
| `configure_index requires at least one of replicas / pod_type` | Called `configure_index` with no args | Pass at least one of `replicas` or `pod_type`. Serverless indexes don't support resize — only pod-based ones do. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'PineconeNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `PineconeNetworkError = PineconeServerError` alias preserved in `exceptions.py`. |
